Title: Informal Armchair Arrangement, Sidewalk
Caption: Photographic record of seating arrangement observed in public space.
Observations: Two upholstered armchairs and a small table are positioned on a rug directly on the sidewalk. The arrangement creates a temporary domestic zone within a public thoroughfare. The seating encourages pause and conversation rather than passage. Its placement blurs the boundary between private comfort and public circulation.

Title: Child-Sized Chair, Private Residence
Caption: Photographic record of child-sized seating.
Observations: Scaled to restrict movement and encourage upright posture. The chair introduces bodily discipline through proportion rather than ornament. Decorative detailing softens but does not diminish its regulatory function. It reflects early social conditioning embedded within domestic space.

Title: Decorative Side Chair, Outdoor Market
Caption: Photographic record of decorative side chair.
Observations: Upholstery featuring a figurative image shifts attention from comfort to visual narrative. The chair presents itself as object before it offers itself as seating. Ornament delays bodily engagement, positioning the sitter in relation to image. Domestic decoration becomes commodity within market space.
Map_Label: SS-003 — Decorative Side Chair, Outdoor Market

Title: Ceremonial Upholstered Chair — Louvre Interior
Caption: Photographic record of ceremonial seating within a museum interior.
Observations: Positioned as part of an interior authority system rather than as usable furniture. Ornamentation and placement prioritize hierarchy and visibility over bodily accommodation. Upholstery implies softness, but access is controlled. Sitting is conditional and ceremonial.
Map_Label: SS-004 — Ceremonial Upholstered Chair — Louvre Interior

Title: Modified Armchair — Exhibition Installation
Caption: Photographic record of seating incorporated into an exhibition installation.
Observations: The chair adopts the form of domestic seating while structurally refusing use. Material intervention disrupts comfort, converting the armchair into an object of ritualized attention. Duration becomes indeterminate; the body is implied but resisted.

Title: Painted Ladder-Back Chair — Commercial Interior
Caption: Photographic record of painted wooden seating observed in a commercial interior.
Observations: Surface wear documents repeated handling and use over time. The chair prioritizes structural stability and minimal accommodation, supporting extended sitting without ergonomic refinement. It functions as durable vernacular seating within a commercial interior.
Map_Label: SS-006 — Painted Ladder-Back Chair — Commercial Interior

Title: Upholstered Armchair — Roadside (Winter)
Caption: Photographic record of upholstered seating observed roadside during winter.
Observations: Domestic upholstered seating is displaced into unmanaged public transit space. Environmental conditions temporarily suspend use, rendering comfort hypothetical rather than accessible. The object remains structurally intact but environmentally compromised.

Title: Artist’s Step Chair — Improvised Seating
Caption: Photographic record of artist’s step chair used as improvised seating.
Observations: Designed to support standing reach rather than seated rest, the object is pressed into improvised sitting. The body compensates for inadequate scale and support, remaining active rather than settled. Duration is structurally limited.
Map_Label: SS-008 — Artist’s Step Chair — Improvised Seating

Title: Reclining Loungers — Hospitality Lawn
Caption: Photographic record of leisure seating aligned for guest use.
Observations: A continuous row of reclining loungers arranged in parallel, facing a shared view. Spacing and orientation are uniform. Although designed for rest, the seating prescribes how leisure is performed—alignment, duration, and relational positioning are structured. Comfort is abundant but organized.

Title: Ornamental Armchairs — Storefront Threshold
Caption: Photographic record of ornamental seating used as storefront display.
Observations: Two ornate chairs frame the threshold between street and interior. Elevated scale and decorative excess suggest ceremonial authority rather than rest. Although positioned in public space, the chairs do not invite occupation. Seating operates symbolically—signaling luxury and hierarchy—while regulating who may cross the threshold and under what posture.

Title: Student Desk-Chair — Classroom Synchronization
Caption: Photographic record of classroom seating during instructional time.
Observations: The desk-chair integrates seating and work surface into a single unit, limiting bodily repositioning. Orientation is fixed toward the front of the room, aligning attention collectively. Scale and rigidity restrict lateral movement; comfort is secondary to endurance. Seating functions as a synchronizing device, holding multiple bodies in parallel states of instructional readiness.

Title: Lakeside Armchairs — Horizon-Oriented Rest
Caption: Photographic record of lakeside balcony seating.
Observations: Seating oriented toward a fixed horizon encourages sustained stillness. Unlike institutional or hospitality arrangements, no collective alignment or duration is prescribed. The body is permitted to remain without instruction, endpoint, or display.

Title: Dining Chairs (Matched Set) — Domestic Displacement
Caption: Photographic record of dining chairs temporarily displaced from interior use.
Observations: A matched set of dining chairs positioned outdoors, removed from their intended interior context. Designed for collective gathering, they remain oriented toward one another without occupants. Their arrangement preserves social possibility while suspending enactment. Seating is prepared but deferred, signaling a pause in domestic routine rather than abandonment.
Map_Label: SS-013 — Dining Chairs (Matched Set) — Domestic Displacement

Title: Lounge Chair — Exhibition Proxy for Rest
Caption: Photographic record of seating presented for viewing rather than use.
Observations: Presented as seating but withdrawn from bodily use, the chair operates as a visual proxy for rest. Its form suggests comfort while denying occupation, integrating into the exhibition’s viewing apparatus. The object becomes something to be looked at rather than inhabited.
Map_Label: SS-014 — Lounge Chair — Exhibition Proxy for Rest

Title: Bentwood Armchair — Retail Seating
Caption: Photographic record of seating that invites pause without encouraging duration.
Observations: A bentwood armchair positioned within a retail environment, offering brief support without encouraging extended occupation. The lightweight frame and cane surfaces suggest permeability and airflow, reinforcing short-duration use. The form accommodates pause during browsing rather than sustained rest, balancing invitation with subtle temporal limitation.

Title: Molded Plywood Lounge Chair — Sidewalk Resale Display
Caption: Photographic record of molded plywood seating displayed for resale.
Observations: A molded plywood lounge chair positioned within a sidewalk resale display. Though structurally capable of supporting extended rest, its placement among stacked and adjacent furniture withdraws it from immediate use. The chair operates as a commodity image rather than a seating instrument, inviting visual evaluation instead of bodily occupation. Comfort is implied but suspended pending purchase.
Map_Label: SS-016 — Molded Plywood Lounge Chair — Sidewalk Resale Display

Title: Deep-Seated Armchair — Domestic Rest
Caption: Photographic record of  a deep-seated armchair designed to support extended rest and informal postures.
Observations: A deep-seated upholstered armchair positioned within a private interior and actively occupied. The form supports extended rest and informal bodily orientation, allowing the occupant to recline and elevate the legs. Unlike retail or display seating, this chair fully accommodates duration, operating as a domestic site of containment and release rather than regulation.
